fixed_basis: pre-calibrated once at phi=pi; no per-step optimization

pre_registered_predictions.P1_gamma1_null: PASS: gamma=1 has no phase-1 write and no later release difference.
pre_registered_predictions.P2_fixed_basis_stress: PASS: stress gamma=0 and gamma=0.25 remain positive without per-step optimized bases.
pre_registered_predictions.P3_strict_positivity: PASS: normal/storage later adaptive activity is not counted positive without phase-1 Bell-excess terrain inscription.
pre_registered_predictions.P4_replay_specificity: PASS: matched replay reproduces post-write dynamics.

safe_interpretation: A fixed-basis finite-shot CHSH measurement boundary, pre-calibrated once rather than optimized per step, can still write terrain memory and shift later adaptive measurement gates in stress context. Normal/storage later adaptive activity is not counted positive without phase-1 Bell-excess terrain inscription. This remains model-level and not hardware or ordinary local population plumbing.
limitations: Not chemical realism or biological metabolism.
The fixed basis is pre-calibrated at the stress/Bell point, not derived from hardware calibration.
Post-write adaptive dynamics follow the written terrain trace and are not independently quantum-specific.
Local population plumbing without measurement-boundary writeback remains classical-effective.
